rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order To Compel The Deposition Of Alfonso Castro
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 5, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDREW PETERSON, SUSAN PETERSON Motion For An Order To Compel The Deposition Of Alfonso Castro. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITION OF ALFONSO CASTRO, ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT ALFONSO CASTRO WILL APPEAR AND SUBMIT TO HIS DEPOSITION AND PRODUCE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, SAVE AS TO A/C PRIVILIGE, THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED IN EXHIBIT "A" IN THE ORIGINAL NOTICE OF DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 22. 2014. IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER HE WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM TESTIFYING AT THE TRIAL OF THIS MATTER UNLESS CALLED TO THE STAND BY PLAINTIFF. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT CASTRO FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2014. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THERABOTANICS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. SOLAZYME, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, September 5, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT SEPHORA S.A. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction The motion to quash is denied. Plaintiff establishes specific jurisdiction and defendant fails to present evidence showing that the exercise of jurisdiction would be unreasonable. Plaintiff shows that "the defendant has purposefully availed himself or herself of forum benefits; (2) the controversy is related to or arises out of the defendant's contacts with the forum; and (3) the assertion of personal jurisdiction would comport with fair play and substantial justice." Pavlovich v. Sup. Crt. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 262, 269. Defendant and Solazyme agreed that defendant would distribute the California products in Europe and the Middle East for 2 years. Defendant availed himself of a California benefit by obtaining access to the California products. The controversy arises from defendant's California contacts because plaintiff allegedly had an exclusive agreement with Solazyme and defendant's conduct allegedly interfered with that agreement. It is undisputed that part of the defendant's contract was to be performed in CA. It is proper for the court to exercise jurisdiction in this case because defendant had continuous contacts with California, purposefully derived a benefit from those activities, and it was fair to require defendant to account in California for the consequences that arose from such activities. Lastly, defendant fails to present any evidence showing how the exercise of jurisdiction is unreasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: TRAVIS SEAN PERRINE VS. RUN FPC, LLC, D/B/A/BAR NONE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Run Fpc, Llc Dba Bar None'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Friday, September 5, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT RUN FPC, LLC, D/B/A/BAR NONE Run FPC, LLC Dba Bar None's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Granted. Pursuant to CCP sec. 473(b), the default is set aside. Defendant has established that it mistakenly believed it was represented by counsel until June 2014. Upon learning that it was not represented by counsel, the evidence establishes that Defendant acted diligently to defend the action - first, by attempting to settle with Plaintiff and then, when a settlement could not be reached, hiring counsel who immediately attempted to file an answer. Defendant may file its proposed answer, attached as Exhibit I to the Pliner Declaration, within 10 days of entry of this Order. Defendant shall pay Plaintiff $600.00 in reasonable attorney's fees incurred in preparing the default paperwork within 30 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

Hamrick, Kenneth Greene'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is overruled as to the first, second, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action, as each sufficiently states facts supporting a claim.
